title: lichen_l2.c: lichen_link_tx NULL destination semantics undocumented at call site
description:
## Location
firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:364

## Issue
The call to lichen_link_tx() passes NULL as the fourth parameter (destination):

```c
ret = lichen_link_tx(&link_ctx, tx_ipv6_buf, pkt_len, NULL,
                     tx_frame_buf, &frame_len);
```

The call site has no comment explaining what NULL means for the destination parameter. The reader must go to lichen/link.h to understand that NULL means broadcast mode.

## What goes wrong
- A contributor modifying this code may not realize NULL triggers broadcast mode
- If lichen_link_tx() semantics change (e.g., NULL becomes an error), this will silently break without any call-site indication of intent
- Pattern will be copied without understanding - future unicast TX code may incorrectly pass NULL

## Fix
Add a comment at the call site:
```c
ret = lichen_link_tx(&link_ctx, tx_ipv6_buf, pkt_len,
                     NULL,  /* dst_eui64: NULL = broadcast */
                     tx_frame_buf, &frame_len);
```
